zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard le Brun Lord of Drumburgh於1305年出生在英國波藍德時，他的父親Robert le Brun21歲，母親Margaret of Drumburgh23歲。他於1324年與Agnes of Bownwys在英國維斯特莫藍德包--文德莫結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1354年在英國英格蘭享年49歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard le Brun Lord of Drumburgh于1305年出生在英国波藍德时，他的父亲Robert le Brun21岁，母亲Margaret of Drumburgh23岁。他于1324年与Agnes of Bownwys在英国維斯特莫藍德包--文德莫结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1354年在英国英格兰享年49岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Brun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Patrice, Philippe, Pierre, Thierry, Armand, Georges, Henri, Jean Michael, Jean-Marc, Lorette, Lucien.

French, Catalan, and Scandinavian; North German (also Brün): nickname from French and Catalan brun, Middle Low German brūn ‘brown’, referring to the color of the hair, complexion, or clothing. As a surname of French origin it is also found in Haiti. Compare French Lebrun and German Bruen .
North German, Scandinavian, and Dutch: from the ancient Germanic personal name Brun(o), from brūna ‘brown’. See also Braun .

variants.name: Brunell
meanings: English (Lancashire): nickname denoting someone with a brownish complexion, from Middle English burnel, a metathesized form of Old French brunel, a diminutive of Old French brun ‘brown’; used also as a personal name.
Swedish: ornamental name from brun ‘brown’ + the suffix -ell, a derivative of the Latin adjectival ending -elius.
German (also Brünell): nickname meaning ‘brown’ (see Brun ).

variants.name: Breunig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Florian, Franz, Math, Siegfried, Winfried.
German: from the old personal name Brūning, a derivative of Brun(o) (see Brun ), which was diphthongized (via Brüning; see Bruning 2) to Breuni(n)g in the 17th century.

variants.name: Brauning
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gernot, Gunther, Helmut.
South German (Bräuning): patronymic from the ancient Germanic personal name Brun(o) (see Brun ).

variants.name: Bruneau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Patrice, Alcide, Amie, Andre, Arianne, Armand, Cecile, Emile, Jean-Claude, Lucien, Napoleon.
French: diminutive of   Brun   ‘brown’. Compare   Bronaugh  .

History: Pierre-Guillaume Bruneau dit Pelletier, from Poitiers, married Marie-Élisabeth Morin in Quebec City, QC, in 1758. Joseph Petit dit Bruneau from Paris, France, married Marie Chenay in Quebec City, QC, in 1675. — This surname is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Breun
meanings: German: from a pet form of the personal name Bruno, see Brun .

variants.name: Bruner
meanings: German: nickname from Old High German, Old Saxon Brūn (see Brun ).
German and Jewish: variant of   Brunner  .

variants.name: Bruhn
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans, Bernd, Bernhard, Dieter, Kurt, Gerhard, Heinz, Johann, Wolfgang.
North German (also Brühn), Danish, and Swedish: variant of Brun .

variants.name: Braun
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Ernst, Otto, Alois, Gerhard, Helmut, Fritz, Heinz, Joerg, Wolfgang, Egon.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname from German braun ‘brown’ (Middle High German brūn), referring to the color of the hair, complexion, or clothing.

German: from the personal name Bruno, which was borne by the Dukes of Saxony, among others, from the 10th century or before. It was also the name of several medieval German and Italian saints, including Saint Bruno, the founder of the Carthusian order (1030–1101), who was born in Cologne. See also Brun .

variants.name: Brunel
meanings: French: diminutive of   Brun   ‘brown’. Compare   Brunelle  .
History: Jacques Brunel from Dieppe in Seine-Maritime, France, married Suzanne Bertault in Boucherville, QC, in 1677.

variants.name: Lebrun
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Fernand, Normand, Andre, Emile, Herve, Lucien, Michel, Yves, Adelard, Dominique, Edouard.
French and Walloon: variant of Brun ‘brown’, with fused masculine definite article le.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard le Brun Lord of Drumburgh was born in 1305, in Cumberland, England, his father, Robert le Brun, was 21 and his mother, Margaret of Drumburgh, was 23. He married Agnes of Bownwys in 1324, in Bowness-on-Windermere, Westmorland, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1354, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard le Brun Lord of Drumburgh est né en 1305, à Cumberland, Angleterre, son père, Robert le Brun, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Margaret of Drumburgh, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Agnes of Bownwys en 1324, à Bowness-on-Windermere, Westmorland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1354, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 49 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard le Brun Lord of Drumburgh 1305 in Cumberland, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert le Brun 21 und seine Mutter Margaret of Drumburgh 23. Er hat 1324 in Bowness-on-Windermere, Westmorland, England Agnes of Bownwys geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1354 im Alter von 49 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard le Brun Lord of Drumburgh è nato il nel 1305 nel Cumberland, England, il suo padre, Robert le Brun, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Margaret of Drumburgh, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Agnes of Bownwys nel 1324, nel Bowness-on-Windermere, Westmorland, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1354, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 49 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard le Brun Lord of Drumburghが1305年にイギリスクウンバーランドで生まれた時，父Robert le Brunは21歳，母Margaret of Drumburghは23歳であった。1324年にイギリスウエストモーランドボウネス-オン-ウィンデルメアでAgnes of Bownwysと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1354年にイギリスイギリスで49歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard le Brun Lord of Drumburgh(이)가 1305년 영국 c벌랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert le Brun(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret of Drumburgh(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1324년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 보운스--윈더머에서 Agnes of Bownwys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1354년 영국 잉글랜드에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard le Brun Lord of Drumburgh nasceu em 1305, em Cumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert le Brun, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Margaret of Drumburgh, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes of Bownwys em 1324, em Bowness-on-Windermere, Westmorland, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1354, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 49 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard le Brun Lord of Drumburgh родился в 1305 г. в местности кумбирланд, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert le Brun было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret of Drumburgh, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes of Bownwys в 1324 г. в местности боунесс-он-виндирмир, вестморланд, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1354 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 49 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard le Brun Lord of Drumburgh nació en 1305, en Cumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert le Brun, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Margaret of Drumburgh, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Agnes of Bownwys en 1324, en Bowness-on-Windermere, Westmorland, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1354, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 49 años.
